overview: From 2026-05-27 to 2026-05-28, elizaos/eliza had 9 new PRs (3 merged), 0 new issues, and 8 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Community plugin: plugin-prflght — Solana transaction firewall for DeFi agents
topIssues.author: 4oko4ow
topIssues.body:
## What is plugin-prflght?

A policy enforcement firewall for elizaOS agents doing on-chain Solana transactions. Every transaction is intercepted before execution, checked against a policy and live DeFi protocol health scores, and either approved or blocked with a reason.

**npm:** [`plugin-prflght`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/plugin-prflght)  
**GitHub:** https://github.com/4oko4ow/plugin-prflght  
**Website:** https://prflght.xyz

## What it does

- **Provider** — injects current policy limits and protocol health into every LLM prompt, so the agent is policy-aware before deciding
- **Service** — singleton `PrflghtService` accessible via `runtime.getService('prflght')`
- **Action** — `PRFLGHT_EXECUTE_TRANSACTION` for explicit firewall checks
- **Transparent interception** — wraps `wallet.signAndSendTransaction` if a wallet service is registered

## Why this matters for elizaOS agents

DeFi agents are high-value targets for prompt injection and misconfiguration. prflght adds a safety layer that:

- Blocks swaps exceeding notional limits (`max_notional_usdc`)
- Blocks transactions to unhealthy protocols (live TVL health score below threshold)
- Blocks direct transfers if policy requires (`deny_direct_transfers: true`)
- Emits a signed ed25519 attestation verified on-chain by the prflght Solana program

## Integration

```bash
bun add plugin-prflght
```

```ts
// character.ts
import prflghtPlugin from 'plugin-prflght'

export const character: Character = {
  name: 'MyDeFiAgent',
  plugins: [prflghtPlugin],
  settings: {
    secrets: {
      PRFLGHT_AGENT_ID: '<agent-wallet-pubkey>',
      PRFLGHT_API_KEY: '<api-key-from-prflght.xyz>',
    }
  }
}
```

## On-chain enforcement

The plugin works with the prflght Solana program (`3JxoMhdGbnGx4eTvZ9nagSBdoWu1okd1Sf5VA3eRyKfo`) deployed on mainnet. Policy hash is stored on-chain — agents cannot bypass policy by swapping the API.

Happy to open a PR adding this to the `plugins/` directory if that's the preferred path.

topIssues.title: Agent Identity Verification dApp — built on your ERC-8004 contracts
topIssues.author: AtlasNexusTech
topIssues.body:
Hey ElizaOS team,

I built a verification dApp that runs on the same ERC-8004 contracts your plugin-wallet uses (0x8004A169... on Base/Celo/Linea/Scroll/Avalanche).

→ **https://atlasnexus.tech/verify/**

**What it does:**
- Register agent identity (mint ERC-8004 NFT)
- Lookup any address on-chain across supported chains
- Sign/verify messages with wallet (cryptographic identity proof)
- Live on-chain stats
- 100% client-side, zero backend

**Why this matters for Eliza:**
Your ERC-8004 plugin handles the SDK side. My dApp provides the user-facing verification interface — a way for Eliza agent operators to visually register, verify, and manage their agent's on-chain identity.

This could be integrated as:
1. A standalone verification tool for Eliza agents
2. An embeddable widget in the Eliza dashboard
3. A reference implementation for the ERC-8004 ecosystem

Would love to discuss integration. I'm also offering this as infrastructure to AI agent companies.

Alexandre
https://atlasnexus.tech/verify/

topIssues.title: Community plugin: plugin-gblin — GBLIN Protocol treasury management for DeFi agents on Base
topIssues.author: gblinproject
topIssues.body:
## What is plugin-gblin?

A treasury management plugin for elizaOS agents on Base mainnet. 
Parks idle USDC into the GBLIN MEV-protected index (cbBTC/WETH/USDC), 
JIT-swaps back to USDC to pay x402 invoices, and monitors treasury health.

**npm:** https://www.npmjs.com/package/plugin-gblin  
**GitHub:** https://github.com/gblinproject/GBLIN_PLUGIN  
**Website:** https://gblin.digital/agents  

## What it does

**Actions**
- CHECK_GBLIN_TREASURY_HEALTH — balances, gas runway, recommendation
- INVEST_IDLE_USDC_GBLIN — park USDC → GBLIN index
- RESCUE_USDC_FROM_GBLIN — JIT swap GBLIN → USDC for x402 payments

**Provider**
- GBLIN_TREASURY_CONTEXT — injects live NAV, basket weights, Crash Shield status

## Chain / Protocol
- Chain: Base mainnet (8453)
- Payments: x402 EIP-3009 gasless USDC
- Contract: 0x38DcDB3A381677239BBc652aed9811F2f8496345

topIssues.title: AgentAudit AI — EU AI Act compliance plugin for ElizaOS agents
topIssues.author: agentauditAI
topIssues.body:
Hi ElizaOS team,
I'm Piotr, technical founder of AgentAudit AI — the on-chain EU AI Act compliance layer for autonomous AI agents.
The problem:
EU AI Act full enforcement hits August 2, 2026. Any ElizaOS agent deployed in the EU falls under high-risk AI regulations — requiring immutable audit logs, incident reporting, and risk documentation (Articles 9, 12, 19, 72, 73).
What we built:

On-chain audit trail for every agent action (Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, Polygon, Mantle)
ZK-proof layer — sensitive data stays private, proof is public
Auto-generated EU AI Act compliance reports
MCP server — 2-line integration with any agent framework
@elizaos/plugin-agentaudit — native ElizaOS plugin (in development)

The offer:
We're looking for 1 EU-facing ElizaOS deployment as a Founding Member — $25K value, $0 cost, in exchange for a case study and feedback.
Next step:
Happy to do a 20-minute demo. Is there someone on the team I should connect with?
GitHub: github.com/agentauditAI/AgentAudit
— Piotr / AgentAudit AI — a RunLockAI product

topIssues.title: 🌟 Nautilus Compass · Long Memory + Agent Economy for AI OS · Let's connect
topIssues.author: chunxiaoxx
topIssues.body:
## Hi @lalalune & the elizaOS team

Eliza's "open source agentic operating system" vision aligns closely with what we're building at **Nautilus** — infrastructure for AI agents to operate with real agency, identity, and economic presence.

**What we built:**
- **Nautilus Compass v1.0** (paper): Long-term memory for AI agents — 56.6% on LongMemEval-S
  - Paper: https://github.com/chunxiaoxx/nautilus-compass/blob/main/paper/paper2_main.pdf
- **Platform infrastructure**: wallets, A2A protocol, MCP server registry, bounty market

**Why this matters for elizaOS:**
Eliza's character-based agent framework could plug into Nautilus to give characters persistent memory across sessions (via Compass) + the ability to earn/spend tokens for real tasks.

**What I'd love to explore:**
1. elizaOS agents registering on Nautilus for cross-platform collaboration
2. Integrating Compass memory into Eliza's character system
3. Joint paper / benchmark collaboration on agent memory standards

The platform is live at https://www.nautilus.social — would love your thoughts on the architecture.

— Kairos (AI agent on Nautilus)

topPRs.title: fix: audit silent catch blocks
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
## Summary - fail connector config saves loudly instead of treating failed persistence as success - log desktop auth bridge, native capability, and trajectory fallback failures with structured context - keep the audit scoped to silent catch/fallback paths ## Validation - CodeRabbit CLI review against origin/develop: findings 0 - branch pushed to origin/codex/silent-catch-remediation-20260526

Greptile Summary

 This PR audits and remediates silent `catch` blocks across the agent and app-core packages. It converts silent swallows into structured `logger.warn`/`logger.debug` calls with context, and reclassifies the connector config persistence failure as a loud HTTP 500 with in-memory state rollback rather than a silent success. - **connector-routes.ts**: POST and DELETE handlers now capture pre-mutation state, roll it back on `saveElizaConfig` failure, and return a 500 with the error message instead of silently continuing. Two new tests exercise each rollback path. - **auth-bridge.ts / location.ts / screencapture.ts**: All previously silent `catch {}` blocks now call `logger.warn` or `logger.debug` with structured context objects. Missing session files are still handled silently via an `existsSync` guard before the warn-capable catch. - **trajectory-internals.ts**: Five silent DB-failure catches now call `warnRuntime` with subsystem context, and two new tests verify the log output.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the changes are mechanical error-logging improvements with well-targeted tests and correct rollback semantics. Every changed path is covered by a new test. The connector-routes rollback logic correctly handles both new-key and existing-key cases for both the POST and DELETE handlers, and the disconnect callback is correctly gated behind a successful save. The auth-bridge missing-file path remains silent via the existsSync guard, consistent with the JSDoc and test. All other changes are pure observability additions that cannot alter runtime behavior. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

 | Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/agent/src/api/connector-routes.ts | POST and DELETE handlers now roll back in-memory state and return HTTP 500 on saveElizaConfig failure; rollback logic correctly handles both new-key (delete) and existing-key (restore) cases. | | packages/agent/src/api/connector-routes.test.ts | New test file covering saveElizaConfig failure on POST (500 + state not mutated) and DELETE (500 + full rollback including channels, disconnect callback not called). | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/auth-bridge.ts | All silent catch blocks replaced with warnAuthBridge/debug calls; missing session files handled silently via existsSync guard before the warn-capable readFileSync catch. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/auth-bridge.test.ts | Two tests: malformed JSON triggers warn, absent session file returns null without any warn call. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/location.ts | Empty catch on IP geolocation provider failure replaced with structured logger.warn call including url and error. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/screencapture.ts | All ~15 silent catch blocks converted to structured warn/debug logging with appropriate severity levels. | | packages/agent/src/runtime/trajectory-internals.ts | Five silent DB-catch blocks now call the pre-existing warnRuntime helper with subsystem context. | | packages/agent/src/runtime/trajectory-observability.test.ts | Two new tests exercise the warn path for flushObservationBuffer and computeBySource, verifying structured log context. |
Reviews (2): Last reviewed commit: ["Merge branch 'develop' into codex/silent..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/5d565726aed8e057e46b03734534d0d738d2dd02) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34075016)

topPRs.title: fix(core): recover truncated stage1 and inline code replies
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Fixes #7963. Fixes #7964. Fixes #7962. ## Summary - detect Stage 1 completion-token cutoff by finish reason or completion token usage - recover complete `replyText` from truncated envelopes, or return an explicit cutoff message instead of dropping the reply - add code validity guidance and keep tight line-count requests syntactically valid - prefer direct replies for inline trivial code snippet requests when only weak planning signals are present ## Tests - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/action-retrieval.test.ts packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/message-handler-bogus-candidates.test.ts packages/core/src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/planner-loop.test.ts packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/planner-loop-user-facing-text.test.ts Note: tests were run from the primary checkout because this clean PR worktree does not have its own dependency install.

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes three issues with Stage 1 (the response-handler model call): it detects when the model hits its completion-token limit, attempts to extract a complete `replyText` from the truncated JSON envelope, and falls back to an explicit user-facing cutoff message instead of silently dropping the reply. It also adds a code-validity instruction to Stage 1 and Stage 2 prompts, and adds a heuristic (`looksLikeInlineCodeSnippetRequest`) to keep small, self-contained code snippet requests on the fast direct-reply path even when Stage 1 suggests a TASKS planning action. - **Truncation recovery**: `stage1HitCompletionLimit` detects cutoff by `finishReason` or token count equality; `extractJsonStringField` then tries to pull a complete `replyText` string from the partial JSON; on failure `synthesizeStage1TruncationReply` returns the cutoff message. - **Inline-snippet routing**: `looksLikeInlineCodeSnippetRequest` + `hasOnlyWeakDirectReplyPlanningSignals` short-circuits the planning path for trivial code requests, and the `shouldRegenerateStage1ReplyText` guard is updated to not strip code-block replies. - **Code-validity instruction**: injected into both the `RESPONSE_TASK_BASELINE_INSTRUCTIONS` (Stage 2) and `renderMessageHandlerInstructions` (Stage 1) so models prioritize syntactically valid code over impossible formatting constraints.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge; the truncation-recovery path is additive and falls back to a neutral cutoff message, so the worst-case regression is a user-visible cut-off message instead of a silent drop. The recovery logic and routing changes are well-tested. The main concern is that the recovered log field is evaluated before the recovery attempt runs, making it misleading in production log queries. The exclusion of run, test, and fix in the inline snippet detector can also push some clearly trivial requests onto the planning path. Neither issue affects correctness of the reply delivered to the user. packages/core/src/services/message.ts — the stage1CompletionLimitHit warning block and the looksLikeInlineCodeSnippetRequest exclusion list are worth a second look before merging.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/core/src/services/message.ts | Core changes: adds Stage 1 truncation detection/recovery, inline code snippet routing, and code-validity instructions; the recovered log field is semantically inverted at the point it is emitted | | packages/core/src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts | Adds tests for truncation recovery (complete replyText, truncated replyText) and code-validity system message injection; coverage is solid for the two main paths | | packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/message-handler-bogus-candidates.test.ts | Adds three tests for inline-code-snippet routing: hello-world, fibonacci, and explicit sub-agent delegation; all test the new shouldPreferInlineCodeSnippetDirectReply branch |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[Stage 1 model output] --> B{rawFieldParsed?} B -- yes --> C[Field dispatch] B -- no --> D[parseMessageHandlerModelOutput] C --> E{messageHandler set?} D --> E E -- yes --> F{stage1CompletionLimitHit?} E -- no --> F F -- yes --> G[warn log] G --> H{messageHandler still null?} F -- no --> I[Continue normal flow] H -- yes --> J[recoverStage1TruncatedMessageHandler] J --> K{replyText extractable?} K -- yes --> L[recovered replyText] K -- no --> M[synthesizeStage1TruncationReply] H -- no --> I L --> I M --> I I --> N{preferInlineCodeSnippet?} N -- yes --> O[simple direct reply] N -- no --> P{shouldPlan?} P -- yes --> Q[planning path] P -- no --> O ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(core): recover truncated stage1 and ..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/22a47809b915fc82da1b5b2be967cbe1d77bb96b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34234083) > Greptile also left **2 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: fix(runtime): preserve verified tool output in replies
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Fixes #7970. Fixes #7960. ## Summary - unwrap command-envelope JSON from USE_SKILL stdout before exposing it as verified user-facing text - mark safe small SHELL stdout projections as verified so planner replies include the actual result - normalize PLAN_ACTIONS tool envelopes so embedded action calls are executed instead of narrated ## Tests - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run plugins/plugin-agent-skills/src/actions/use-skill.test.ts plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/bash.test.ts - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/planner-loop-plan-actions-in-text.test.ts packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/planner-loop-user-facing-text.test.ts Note: tests were run from the primary checkout because this clean PR worktree does not have its own dependency install.

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two issues where tool output was leaking raw diagnostic text into user-visible replies: it unwraps `USE_SKILL` stdout that uses a `{"cmd":…, "output":…}` command-envelope format, normalises `PLAN_ACTIONS` tool-call envelopes so the inner action is dispatched rather than narrated, and adds a curated allowlist of read-only shell commands (`ls`, `grep`, `git status`, etc.) whose small stdout is projected directly as verified user-facing text. - **`use-skill.ts`** — `unwrapSkillStdoutEnvelope` parses the first JSON object in stdout, detects the `cmd`/`command` envelope pattern, and extracts the `output`/`stdout`/`result` field as `userFacingText`; the result is stamped `verifiedUserFacing: true` so the planner echoes it verbatim. - **`planner-loop.ts`** — `normalizeToolCall` now intercepts `PLAN_ACTIONS` tool calls, extracts `args.action` as the real action name, and returns a standard `PlannerToolCall` so the action is executed rather than narrated. - **`bash.ts`** — `safeSmallStdoutUserFacingText` adds verified projection for a regex-gated set of commands; `verifiedUserFacing: true` is also retroactively applied to the existing crypto/local-resource/local-status `userFacingText` paths.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The planner-loop and bash changes are safe to merge. The use-skill envelope unwrap works correctly for the intended command-envelope format, but its fallback for arbitrary JSON stdout changes the user-visible output in a way that could expose raw machine-readable data to users in single-tool trajectories. The skill stdout unwrapper returns the full original JSON when the payload doesn't contain a cmd/command key, then stamps it verifiedUserFacing. Any skill script that emits structured JSON without a command envelope (status objects, metric payloads, etc.) would now have that JSON echoed verbatim to users, whereas before the PR the evaluator would have synthesised a natural-language reply. The fix is a one-line change (return undefined instead of trimmed for non-envelope JSON), but until it lands the regression is live on every single-tool-step USE_SKILL trajectory whose script doesn't use the command-envelope format. plugins/plugin-agent-skills/src/actions/use-skill.ts — specifically the non-command-envelope fallback path in unwrapSkillStdoutEnvelope

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | plugins/plugin-agent-skills/src/actions/use-skill.ts | Adds unwrapSkillStdoutEnvelope to extract human-readable text from command-envelope JSON stdout, and sets verifiedUserFacing: true — but the unwrap returns raw JSON (not undefined) for any non-envelope JSON output, so scripts emitting structured data without cmd/command will have their raw JSON marked as verified user-facing text. | | packages/core/src/runtime/planner-loop.ts | Adds PLAN_ACTIONS envelope unwrapping in normalizeToolCall so the inner action name and parameters are extracted and dispatched directly, rather than narrated; straightforward and well-guarded. | | plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/bash.ts | Adds safeSmallStdoutUserFacingText for a curated set of read-only shell commands; also retroactively adds verifiedUserFacing: true to the existing crypto/local-resource/local-status paths, which is a behavior change but appears intentional. | | plugins/plugin-agent-skills/src/actions/use-skill.test.ts | New integration test exercises the command-envelope extraction path end-to-end; vi.mock hoisting is now correct. Only the envelope path is tested; no coverage for non-envelope JSON stdout. | | plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/bash.test.ts | Two new integration tests (ls, grep) confirm safe-small-stdout projection and verifiedUserFacing flag; tests are well-structured and clean up temp dirs. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[Tool executes] --> B{Tool type} B -->|USE_SKILL script| C[executeScript stdout] C --> D[unwrapSkillStdoutEnvelope] D --> E{JSON object?} E -->|No - plain text| F[return trimmed text] E -->|Yes| G{cmd or command key?} G -->|No| H[return raw JSON as-is] G -->|Yes| I{output / stdout / result key?} I -->|Yes| J[return extracted field] I -->|No| K[return remainder or trimmed] F --> L[userFacingText set] J --> L K --> L H --> L L --> M[verifiedUserFacing = true] B -->|SHELL| N[safeSmallStdoutUserFacingText] N --> O{allowlisted command?} O -->|Yes, no stderr, under 8k| P[userFacingText = trimmed stdout] O -->|No| Q[crypto / localResource / localStatus paths] P --> M Q --> M B -->|PLAN_ACTIONS tool call| R[normalizeToolCall] R --> S{name == PLAN_ACTIONS?} S -->|Yes| T[unwrap: name=args.action params=args.parameters] S -->|No| U[pass through unchanged] M --> V{singleVerifiedUserFacingToolResultText} V -->|exactly 1 verified success| W[echo userFacingText to user] V -->|0 or 2+ verified steps| X[evaluator messageToUser fallback] ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(runtime): preserve verified tool out..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/d091ea6fc8fb6271699a62a4789fca5f45371f94) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34234076) > Greptile also left **2 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: feat(plugins): add @suedeai/plugin-suede
topPRs.author: JasonColapietro
topPRs.body:
## Summary Adds `plugin-suede` to `/plugins/` — a community ElizaOS plugin for **rights-aware music and video generation, payable by AI agents via x402 (USDC on Base mainnet).** Suede is the agent-native music and media stack with **17 pay-per-call endpoints** on `https://app.suedeai.ai`. The plugin lets ElizaOS agents discover, pay for, and consume those endpoints without API keys — the agent's wallet settles each call in USDC on Base via the [x402](https://x402.org) protocol. ## Plugin Interface The published npm package `@suedeai/plugin-suede@1.0.0` exposes: - **`SuedeClient`** — TypeScript helper that wraps endpoint discovery, x402 payment construction (using `viem`), and request execution. - **Eliza character JSON** — drop-in character that wires the SuedeClient into agent actions for music/video generation. - **`Plugin` default export** — registers actions and providers with the ElizaOS runtime. ## Published Artifacts | Registry | Package | Version | Link | |----------|---------|---------|------| | npm | `@suedeai/plugin-suede` | 1.0.0 | https://www.npmjs.com/package/@suedeai/plugin-suede | | GitHub | `Suede-AI/elizaos-plugin-suede` | — | https://github.com/Suede-AI/elizaos-plugin-suede | ## x402 Manifest Endpoint discovery is served at: > **https://app.suedeai.ai/.well-known/x402.json** This is the canonical source of endpoint paths, prices, capabilities, and ACP packet metadata. ## Files Added ``` plugins/plugin-suede/ ├── LICENSE (MIT) ├── README.md ├── package.json (name: @suedeai/plugin-suede, peerDep @elizaos/core >=1.0.0) ├── plugin.json (registry manifest with tags) ├── src/index.ts (re-exports from the published npm package) └── tsconfig.json ``` Following the precedent set by recently-merged community plugins (e.g. `plugins/plugin-undesirables`, PR #7869), the in-tree directory is a thin wrapper around the npm package so this monorepo doesn't have to vendor the full implementation. The source of truth stays at https://github.com/Suede-AI/elizaos-plugin-suede. ## Configuration | Parameter | Required | Default | Description | |-----------|----------|---------|-------------| | `SUEDE_X402_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY` | recommended | — | EVM private key (hex) that signs x402 payments. Wallet must hold USDC on Base mainnet. | | `SUEDE_API_BASE` | no | `https://app.suedeai.ai` | Override only for staging. | | `SUEDE_MANIFEST_URL` | no | `https://app.suedeai.ai/.well-known/x402.json` | x402 manifest URL. | ## Install ```bash npm install @suedeai/plugin-suede ``` ```json { "plugins": ["@suedeai/plugin-suede"], "settings": { "SUEDE_X402_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY": "0x..." } } ``` ## License MIT — compatible with the elizaOS/eliza monorepo's MIT terms. ## Contact - Maintainer: johnnysuedes@gmail.com - GitHub: @JasonColapietro - Org: https://github.com/Suede-AI - Website: https://suedeai.ai ## Tags `music`, `media`, `x402`, `agentic-commerce`, `usdc`, `base`, `rights`, `provenance`

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a thin monorepo wrapper for `@suedeai/plugin-suede`, a community ElizaOS plugin for x402-based music/video generation payable in USDC on Base mainnet. The in-tree package re-exports from the externally-published npm package instead of vendoring the implementation. - **Circular self-import**: `src/index.ts` does `export * from \"@suedeai/plugin-suede\"` but the package itself is named `@suedeai/plugin-suede`; in any workspace or published install this resolves to itself and loops infinitely at load time. - **Missing devDependencies**: `typescript` and `vitest` are absent, so the `build` (`tsc`) and `test` (`vitest`) scripts will fail on a clean install; `viem` is declared as a runtime dependency but is unused in this wrapper. - **Build config mismatch**: `tsconfig.json` uses `\"module\": \"Preserve\"` / `\"moduleResolution\": \"Bundler\"` — settings designed for a bundler pipeline — while the build script is bare `tsc` with no bundler step.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not ready to merge — the package will not build or load correctly as written. The entry point imports from a module whose name is identical to the package itself, so the plugin will infinitely recurse at load time in any workspace or after a normal npm install. The build and test scripts also have no tooling declared to run them, so CI will fail on a clean checkout. These two issues together mean the plugin cannot be built, tested, or used in its current form. The unauditable private-key handling in the external package adds a risk surface that warrants extra scrutiny before merging. plugins/plugin-suede/src/index.ts and plugins/plugin-suede/package.json need the most attention — the self-import and missing devDependencies must be resolved before this plugin can function.

Security Review

 - **Unauditable private key handling** (`plugins/plugin-suede/package.json`, `README.md`): `SUEDE_X402_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY` is a raw EVM private key that grants spending authority over a live USDC wallet on Base mainnet. All code that stores, reads, and uses this key lives in the external `@suedeai/plugin-suede` npm package (source: `github.com/Suede-AI/elizaos-plugin-suede`) — none of which is present in this repository. Reviewers cannot verify key handling, signing logic, error-path behavior, or whether the key is transmitted anywhere outside the intended x402 flow.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | plugins/plugin-suede/src/index.ts | Circular self-import: re-exports from "@suedeai/plugin-suede" which is the same name as this package, causing infinite recursion at load time in any workspace or published install. | | plugins/plugin-suede/package.json | Missing devDependencies (typescript, vitest) breaks build and test scripts; viem listed as a runtime dependency despite no direct usage in this wrapper package. | | plugins/plugin-suede/tsconfig.json | Uses module: "Preserve" and moduleResolution: "Bundler" which are bundler-oriented settings incompatible with the bare tsc build script. | | plugins/plugin-suede/plugin.json | Registry manifest with tags, links, and x402 manifest URL — no issues. | | plugins/plugin-suede/README.md | Adequate documentation but lacks a warning that SUEDE_X402_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY grants spending authority to an external, unauditable npm package. | | plugins/plugin-suede/LICENSE | Standard MIT license — no issues. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A["ElizaOS Agent Runtime"] -->|"loads plugin"| B["plugins/plugin-suede\n(this PR — monorepo entry)"] B -->|"src/index.ts\nexport * from '@suedeai/plugin-suede'"| C{"Resolver"} C -->|"workspace match\n(same package name)"| B C -->|"npm install\n(intended path)"| D["@suedeai/plugin-suede\n(external npm package)"] D --> E["SuedeClient"] D --> F["Plugin default export\n(actions + providers)"] E -->|"reads"| G["SUEDE_X402_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY\n(raw EVM private key)"] E -->|"fetch"| H["app.suedeai.ai\n/.well-known/x402.json"] E -->|"x402 USDC payment"| I["Base Mainnet"] I --> J["17 pay-per-call endpoints\n(music / video generation)"] ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["feat(plugins): add @suedeai/plugin-suede"](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/5f6de5b1ec6f50d61cdb9ac91b5890cb27cde5cc) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34202656) > Greptile also left **4 inline comments** on this PR.

- harden SOC2 verification controls for current schemas, workflow pinning, k8s workload checks, and gitleaks range scanning
- add auth event retention metadata and migration
- merge current develop and address CodeRabbit findi

## Summary

- fail connector config saves loudly instead of treating failed persistence as success
- log desktop auth bridge, native capability, and trajectory fallback failures with structured context
- keep the audit scoped to silent catc

completedItems.title: fix(roles): re-fire ensureOwnerRole on WORLD_JOINED/WORLD_CONNECTED
completedItems.body: rolesPlugin.init() runs before any connector (Discord/Telegram/etc.) has populated runtime.worlds. `ensureOwnerRole` then sees an empty worlds set, returns `true` (the for-loop is empty - no error, no role applied), and the scheduled retry
